county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Forest County formed 1885 from Langlade and Oconto counties. County seat is Crandon. Northern Wisconsin forest county. Forest County Potawatomi Community and Mole Lake Sokaogon Chippewa reservations. Logging heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: Forest County Register of Deeds in Crandon holds land records from 1885. Northern Wisconsin county with significant Native American presence -- Potawatomi (Forest County Potawatomi Community), Mole Lake Sokaogon Chippewa. Tribal enrollment and allotment records at NARA Great Lakes Region. Logging industry records at WHS. German and Polish settlers also established communities. WHS has microfilmed Forest County records. The Forest County Historical Society in Crandon maintains local collections. Check Langlade and Oconto counties for pre-1885 records. FamilySearch has limited Forest County records. BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Forest County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Crandon; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent survival from 1885; no major record losses

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Grant County formed 1836 from Iowa County. County seat is Lancaster. Southwestern Wisconsin lead mining region. 'Badger State' nickname from lead miners. Cornish, Welsh, Irish, and German settlement. Nelson Dewey, first governor, from Grant County.
county_metadata.research_tips: Grant County Register of Deeds in Lancaster holds land records from 1836. Southwestern Wisconsin lead mining region -- the 'Badger State' nickname originated from lead miners who burrowed into hillsides like badgers in the 1820s-1830s. Cornish, Welsh, Irish, and German miners settled here. The Grant County Historical Society in Lancaster maintains extensive collections. WHS has microfilmed Grant County records. Lead mining records at WHS document miners and families. Platteville Mining Museum. British Isles immigration records are particularly important. Nelson Dewey State Park preserves the first governor's estate. FamilySearch has indexed Grant County records. BLM GLO patents.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Grant County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Lancaster; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent survival from 1836; no major record losses

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Green County formed 1836 from Iowa County. County seat is Monroe. Southern Wisconsin county famous for Swiss settlement at New Glarus (1845). Norwegian settlements also significant. 'Swiss Cheese Capital of the USA.'
county_metadata.research_tips: Green County Register of Deeds in Monroe holds land records from 1836. Southern Wisconsin county famous for Swiss and Norwegian immigrant communities. New Glarus ('America's Little Switzerland') settled in 1845 by Swiss colonists from Canton Glarus. The Swiss Historical Village in New Glarus has immigration and settlement records. Norwegian settlements at Wiota and other communities. The Green County Historical Society in Monroe maintains local collections. WHS has microfilmed Green County records. Swiss Reformed Church and Norwegian Lutheran church records are critical. Monroe is the 'Swiss Cheese Capital of the USA.' FamilySearch has indexed Green County records. BLM GLO patents.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Green County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Monroe; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Green Lake County formed 1858 from Marquette County. County seat is Green Lake. Central Wisconsin county with resort heritage. Mixed German, Norwegian, English, and Irish settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: Green Lake County Register of Deeds in Green Lake holds land records from 1858. Created from Marquette County -- check Marquette County for pre-1858 records. Central Wisconsin county with diverse immigrant settlement (German, Norwegian, English, Irish). WHS has microfilmed Green Lake County records. The Green Lake County Historical Society maintains local collections. Dartford Historical Society in Green Lake has village-era records. Green Lake (the lake itself) was an early resort destination -- hotel guest records document visitors. Church records (Lutheran, Catholic, Methodist) supplement civil records. FamilySearch has some Green Lake County records. BLM GLO patents.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Green Lake County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Green Lake; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Iowa County formed 1829 from Crawford County, predating Wisconsin statehood. County seat is Dodgeville. Major southwestern Wisconsin lead mining county. Mineral Point was a center of Cornish mining settlement. Pendarvis historic site.
county_metadata.research_tips: Iowa County Register of Deeds in Dodgeville holds land records from 1829 -- one of the earliest organized counties in Wisconsin, predating statehood. Named for the Iowa people. Major lead mining county in the southwestern mineral district. Cornish, Welsh, and Irish miners, then German and Norwegian farmers. The Iowa County Historical Society in Dodgeville maintains extensive collections. WHS has microfilmed Iowa County records. Governor William Henry Dodge's papers at WHS. Mineral Point was a major lead mining center -- Pendarvis historic site preserves Cornish miners' homes. Shake Rag Street historic district. FamilySearch has indexed Iowa County records. BLM GLO patents.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Iowa County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Dodgeville; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Iron County formed 1893 from Ashland County. County seat is Hurley. Northernmost county tier, Gogebic Range iron mining. Finnish, Italian, Polish, Swedish, and Cornish settlement. Hurley was a notorious boomtown.
county_metadata.research_tips: Iron County Register of Deeds in Hurley holds land records from 1893. Created from Ashland County -- check Ashland County for pre-1893 records. Iron mining (Gogebic Range) and logging brought Finnish, Italian, Polish, Swedish, and Cornish workers. Hurley was a notorious boomtown. WHS has microfilmed Iron County records. The Iron County Historical Society in Hurley maintains local collections. Mining company employment records at WHS and Michigan Technological University Archives. Finnish temperance society records document community life. FamilySearch has limited Iron County records. BLM GLO patents.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Iron County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Hurley; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Jackson County formed 1853 from La Crosse County. County seat is Black River Falls. Western Wisconsin lumber and farming county. Ho-Chunk (Winnebago) Nation presence significant. Norwegian, German, and Czech settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: Jackson County Register of Deeds in Black River Falls holds land records from 1853. Black River Falls was a lumber industry center. The Ho-Chunk (Winnebago) Nation has significant presence in Jackson County -- tribal records at NARA Great Lakes Region. Norwegian, German, and Bohemian (Czech) immigrants settled in the farming areas. WHS has microfilmed Jackson County records. The Jackson County Historical Society in Black River Falls maintains local collections. Photographer Charles Van Schaick's photographs (at WHS) document the community extensively. FamilySearch has some Jackson County records indexed. BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Jackson County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Black River Falls; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Jefferson County formed 1836 from Milwaukee County. County seat is Jefferson. Southeastern Wisconsin county with strong German settlement. Watertown: first kindergarten in America (Margarethe Schurz, 1856). Carl Schurz connection.
county_metadata.research_tips: Jefferson County Register of Deeds in Jefferson holds land records from 1836. One of the earliest organized counties. Very strong German immigrant settlement (Lutheran and Catholic). Watertown was home to the first kindergarten in America (established by Margarethe Schurz, 1856). Carl Schurz (US Senator, Secretary of the Interior) lived in Watertown -- papers at Library of Congress and WHS. The Jefferson County Historical Society maintains collections. Hoard Historical Museum in Fort Atkinson holds Hoard's Dairyman records and local materials. WHS has microfilmed Jefferson County records. German-language newspaper records at WHS. FamilySearch has indexed Jefferson County records. BLM GLO patents.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Jefferson County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Jefferson; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Juneau County formed 1856 from Adams County. County seat is Mauston. Central Wisconsin county. Camp Douglas military installation. Dells of the Wisconsin River area. German, Norwegian, and Czech settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: Juneau County Register of Deeds in Mauston holds land records from 1856. Created from Adams County -- check Adams County for pre-1856 records. Central Wisconsin county with diverse settlement including German, Norwegian, and Bohemian (Czech) families. Camp Douglas military installation records. The Juneau County Historical Society in Mauston maintains local collections. WHS has microfilmed Juneau County records. The Dells of the Wisconsin River area tourism records. Church records (Lutheran, Catholic, Methodist) supplement civil records. FamilySearch has some Juneau County records. BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries. Volk Field Air National Guard Base records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Juneau County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Mauston; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

source_profiles.repository: Juneau County Register of Deeds, Mauston
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original deed books for Juneau County from 1856; grantor/grantee indexes; mortgages and liens; Register of Deeds office maintains land records. WHS has microfilm of historical deed books
source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent survival from 1856; no major record losses

source_profiles.source_name: Juneau County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal land patents (preemption, homestead, cash entry, military bounty) for Juneau County; Wisconsin is a public land state and GLO patents document original federal land transfers to private ownership

source_profiles.source_name: Juneau County Probate Court Records
source_profiles.repository: Juneau County Probate Court, Mauston
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, inventories, guardianships, and estate settlements for Juneau County from 1856; Probate Court has jurisdiction over estate matters; WHS has microfilm of historical probate records

source_profiles.source_name: Juneau County Marriage Records -- County Clerk / WHS / FamilySearch
source_profiles.repository: Juneau County Clerk, Mauston / Wisconsin Historical Society, Madison
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage records for Juneau County from 1856; County Clerk issued marriage licenses in Wisconsin; WHS holds some county marriage records on microfilm; FamilySearch has indexed Wisconsin marriages; state vital records registration began 1907 (sporadic earlier compliance)

source_profiles.source_name: Juneau County Federal and State Census Records
source_profiles.repository: NARA / FamilySearch / Ancestry / WHS
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census 1860-1950 for Juneau County; Wisconsin state census 1855, 1865, 1875, 1885, 1895, 1905 at WHS (unusually detailed -- include place of birth, occupation, citizenship status); territorial census 1836 and 1842 may include Juneau County entries

source_profiles.source_name: Juneau County Historic Newspapers -- WHS
source_profiles.repository: Wisconsin Historical Society, Madison
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Historic newspapers for Juneau County towns at WHS on microfilm and digital; legal notices, obituaries, marriage announcements, estate settlements, and community news; WHS has one of the nation's largest newspaper collections

source_profiles.source_name: Juneau County Cemetery Records -- Find A Grave / WI GenWeb
source_profiles.repository: Find A Grave / Wisconsin GenWeb / Juneau County Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Cemetery transcriptions for Juneau County including church cemeteries, municipal cemeteries, and pioneer family burial grounds; Find A Grave has growing coverage; Wisconsin GenWeb and local historical societies have published transcriptions; DAR chapters have documented many Wisconsin cemeteries

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Kenosha County formed 1850 from Racine County. County seat is Kenosha (originally Southport). Southeastern Wisconsin industrial county on Lake Michigan. Automobile manufacturing heritage (Nash/Rambler/AMC). Italian, German, and Polish communities.
county_metadata.research_tips: Kenosha County Register of Deeds in Kenosha holds land records from 1850. Created from Racine County -- check Racine County for pre-1850 records. Southeastern Wisconsin county on Lake Michigan. Kenosha (originally Southport) grew as an industrial city -- automobile manufacturing (Nash/Rambler/AMC) brought diverse workers. Italian, German, and Polish immigrant communities are strong. The Kenosha County Historical Society and Kenosha Public Library local history collection. WHS has microfilmed Kenosha County records. Simmons Island and harbor records. Catholic and Lutheran parish records essential. FamilySearch has indexed some Kenosha County records. City directories from the 1870s.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO federal land patents for Kenosha County; county deed books at the Register of Deeds office in Kenosha; WHS has microfilm of historical deed books

source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent survival from 1850; no major record losses

source_profiles.coverage_notes: City directories for Kenosha and other Kenosha County cities; directories list residents by name, address, and occupation; essential for tracking family movements, occupations, and residential patterns; WHS and local libraries hold extensive runs
